summary: Matched the fighter boss library by replacing asm/melee/ft/ftbosslib.s with src/melee/ft/ftbosslib.c and wiring the C object into obj_files.mk. The new C covers Master Hand/Crazy Hand helper logic, movement and velocity helpers, boss-state queries, attribute accessors, SFX selection, and stage/player side effects. Supporting header work added ftbosslib.h, expanded MasterHandAttributes fields through x178, added an inline HSD_JObjSetRotation helper, corrected several ftbosslib-related function prototypes, and added #pragma once to multiple headers. There were no PR body notes or review comments in the slice.
what_changed: Added a 417-line C implementation at src/melee/ft/ftbosslib.c and changed obj_files.mk from asm/melee/ft/ftbosslib.s.o to src/melee/ft/ftbosslib.c.o.

Implemented ftbosslib functions including func_8015BD20, func_8015BD24, func_8015BDB4, func_8015BE40, func_8015BF74, func_8015C010, func_8015C09C, func_8015C190, func_8015C208, func_8015C244, func_8015C270, func_8015C2A8, func_8015C2E0, func_8015C31C, func_8015C358, func_8015C3A0, func_8015C3E8, func_8015C44C, func_8015C4C4, func_8015C530, func_8015C5F8, func_8015C6BC, func_8015C74C, func_8015C7EC, func_8015C88C, func_8015C92C, func_8015C9CC, func_8015CA6C, func_8015CB7C, func_8015CB9C, and func_8015CC14.
Added src/melee/ft/ftbosslib.h with declarations for func_8015C3E8 and func_8015C44C.
Expanded MasterHandAttributes: x14 changed from f32 to s32, and the previous filler at 0x160-0x17C was replaced with named fields x160, x164, x168, x16C, x170, x174, and f32 x178.
Added inline HSD_JObjSetRotation in src/sysdolphin/baselib/jobj.h, using assert_line(618, jobj), assigning jobj->rotate, and marking the matrix dirty unless flag 0x02000000 is set.
Updated include/functions.h prototypes so func_8015C31C and func_8015C3A0 return BOOL and func_8015C3E8 takes s32 instead of u32.
Added #pragma once to include/variables.h, src/dolphin/os/init/__start.h, and multiple fighter character headers.
smart_moves: Used a local my_sqrtf implementation with __frsqrte refinement to reproduce the assembly sequence for vector length instead of relying on a library sqrtf that would likely produce different codegen.
Added get_zero() as a documented float reorder hack for func_8015BF74, showing a targeted solution to instruction ordering rather than broad rewrites.
Preserved stack and register behavior with explicit unused locals such as s32 unused arrays and volatile float y in my_sqrtf.
Split some logic into inline helpers, such as func_8015CA6C_inline and func_8015CB9C_inline, likely to match call structure and code layout while keeping source readable.

Modeled HSD_JObjSetRotation as an inline baselib helper with the same assert and dirty-matrix semantics visible in the deleted assembly, making future callers reusable instead of open-coding JObj rotation writes.
Introduced only minimal ftbosslib.h declarations needed by other code, avoiding overclaiming names for still-unknown functions.
decomp_lessons: For asm-to-C matching, the build-system swap in obj_files.mk is part of the match: replacing an asm object with a C object should be committed with the new source.
When matching math-heavy PowerPC code, reproducing the compiler's reciprocal-square-root refinement can be necessary; normal sqrtf or vector helper calls may not match.
Small float constants and comparisons can require helper functions or volatile temporaries to force the same instruction order.
Struct filler should be converted into named fields only when offsets are exercised by matched code; this PR exposes MasterHandAttributes fields x160 through x178 because ftbosslib reads them.
Existing project naming for unknown symbols remains address-based func_XXXXXXXX, while comments can document suspected semantics such as get_fighter_gobj(FighterKind) without renaming prematurely.
Header hygiene changes like adding #pragma once can be necessary collateral when new C includes older character headers.
naming_conventions: Unknown functions retained address names such as func_8015C3E8 and func_8015C44C.

Comments were used to record likely semantics without committing to a rename, e.g. func_8015C3E8(s32 arg0) has the comment get_fighter_gobj(FighterKind), and func_8015C44C has the comment Fighter_GetState(FighterKind).
Struct fields follow offset-style names such as x160, x164, x178 in MasterHandAttributes.
Fighter fields use existing offset names such as x80_self_vel, xB0_pos, x2C_facing_direction, x10C_ftData, and x221F_flag.bits.b3.
The code uses existing fighter kind constants including FTKIND_MASTERH and FTKIND_CREZYH; the apparent CREZYH spelling is preserved from the codebase rather than normalized.
The local enum Gm_PKind uses names Gm_PKind_Human, Gm_PKind_Cpu, Gm_PKind_Demo, Gm_PKind_NA, and Gm_PKind_Boss.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Deleted ftbosslib.s only after adding a C replacement and changing the object list to compile src/melee/ft/ftbosslib.c.o.
Used static inline my_sqrtf and my_lbvector_Len to match the deleted assembly's frsqrte-based length calculation.
Used a volatile float in my_sqrtf and unused local variables/arrays to influence stack layout and emitted code.
Used get_zero() to force float comparison/reorder behavior in func_8015BF74.
Implemented HSD_JObjSetRotation inline so func_8015C09C could express the rotation update while matching the original assert/dirty-matrix behavior.
Kept assertion strings and line numbers in source where visible from assembly, e.g. __assert("ftbosslib.c", 103, ...), and assert_line(618, jobj).
Used integer and floating constants directly where they correspond to assembly behavior, including M_PI for the rotation helper and SFX IDs 0x4E21A through 0x4E21D.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that rename or document ftbosslib functions func_8015C3E8 and func_8015C44C.
Search for subsequent cleanup of the TODO struct around lbl_804D782C in ftbosslib.c.
Search for later replacement of my_sqrtf or the comment about fixing sqrtf.
Find later PRs that expand or rename MasterHandAttributes fields x160 through x178.
Search for additional use sites of HSD_JObjSetRotation after this PR.
